title: Solid Snake
subtitle: FOXHOUND operative / infiltration specialist
summary: Legendary stealth operative used as the main reference for the Classic Codec and Side Ops prototype.
details: Built around infiltration discipline: avoid detection, use equipment carefully, preserve mission options.
In this app, Solid Snake is the default Side Ops player identity for the Shadow Moses-era prototype.
Codec support should stay mission-focused: objective reminders, alert warnings, item hints, and boss advice.
timeline: MSX era through later Solid Snake operations

title: Roy Campbell
subtitle: Mission command and tactical support
summary: Primary mission support contact for multiple classic-era simulations.
details: Campbell’s role is to keep the player aligned with the mission objective and warn against unnecessary exposure.
The app maps him to mission commander support in both MSX/MG2 and Shadow Moses visual packs.
Side Ops uses Campbell as the default trigger voice for mission_start, suspicion, alert and mission_complete.

title: Mei Ling
subtitle: Save contact and memory support
summary: Shadow Moses-era save/memory contact used by the app as the local simulation save anchor.
details: The app uses Mei Ling to explain local save state, memory contacts and call history.
Her module is intentionally separated from tactical command so save/contact memory can evolve independently.
Future passes can connect her to proverb-style flavor, save slots and mission replay comments.

title: Hal Emmerich / Otacon
subtitle: Technical support, systems and robotics specialist
summary: Technical Codec contact used for cameras, locked systems, robotics and digital support packs.
details: Otacon is best used when the player interacts with electronics, surveillance, remote systems or hangar technology.
The app includes MGS1 and MGS2-flavored support entries so the same role can adapt per era.
Future Side Ops missions can use him to explain terminals, drone doors, turret blind spots and equipment upgrades.

title: Naomi Hunter
subtitle: Medical, genetics and nanomachine support
summary: Medical support contact used for low-health triggers and biological/intel explanations.
details: Naomi’s in-app role is to explain damage, rations, stress and low-health behavior.
Her support should be reserved for medical or biological context instead of generic mission hints.
The Side Ops low_health trigger can be expanded into detailed injury state comments later.

title: Naked Snake / Big Boss
subtitle: Survival-era field operative and future legend
summary: Core identity for MGS3-style survival radio packs and future jungle Side Ops missions.
details: The MGS3 radio pack should focus on survival, camouflage, food, injuries, weapon handling and field mentor advice.
Future missions can use separate support voices for mission command, medical save, weapon technique and mentor guidance.
This entry helps prevent mixing Shadow Moses Codec behavior into the survival radio era.

title: Raiden
subtitle: Big Shell field operative / digital support subject
summary: Ideal player identity for future MGS2 digital Codec and VR-heavy training paths.
details: Raiden fits the VR Missions module because his context strongly supports training simulation logic.
MGS2-style calls should separate mission protocol from personal support and digital identity pressure.
Future missions can branch between Tanker-style Snake support and Plant-style Raiden support.

title: Venom Snake
subtitle: MGSV field commander simulation identity
summary: Default anchor for future MGSV/iDroid missions, cassette logs and open Side Ops variants.
details: The MGSV pack should be driven more by iDroid, tapes and mission intel than by classic frequency tuning.
Future gameplay can add extraction, outpost capture and Mother Base recovery loops.
The current app uses simulated channels instead of pretending every MGSV contact has a classic Codec frequency.

title: FOXHOUND
subtitle: Special forces unit and classic Codec support identity
summary: Central organization reference for classic Snake operations, support contacts and the rank vocabulary.
details: The app uses FOXHOUND as both lore organization and rank flavor: Hound, Fox, Foxhound, Big Boss.
It should remain tied to classic operations and not be used as a generic label for every faction.
Future databases can distinguish unit history, personnel, defectors and simulation-only extensions.

title: Outer Heaven
subtitle: Militarized fortress state concept
summary: MSX-era reference for retro radio operations and future fortress infiltration packs.
details: Outer Heaven should drive MSX-style missions: simple radio UI, fortress rooms, item progression and classic infiltration loops.
The current MSX pack is only a visual/radio foundation; real MSX-style maps can be added later.
Keep it visually distinct from Shadow Moses to avoid collapsing all eras into one green Codec skin.

title: Patriots AI Layer
subtitle: Corrupted simulation / meta-communication mode
summary: Glitch mode used to create unstable calls, fake protocol messages and meta anomalies without damaging the real app state.
details: This app treats Patriots AI as a visual and narrative corruption layer, not as a destructive system feature.
Useful effects include wrong speaker labels, repeated mission text, corrupted signals and abnormal CALL timing.
All corruption should remain cosmetic/local and reversible from Settings.

title: Shadow Moses
subtitle: Snowbound nuclear disposal facility context
summary: Primary visual and mission reference for the first Side Ops vertical slice.
details: The current Dock Infiltration mission is a simulation inspired by Shadow Moses-style entry logic, not a direct map recreation.
Its palette should stay cold, industrial and military: snow, dock metal, security lights and hangar approach.
Future mission packs can branch into heliport, tank hangar, nuclear storage, labs and tower-like zones.

title: Big Shell
subtitle: Offshore facility / digital Codec context
summary: Future location pack for MGS2-style digital support, platform traversal and identity-heavy VR logic.
details: Big Shell missions should emphasize struts, bridges, cameras, water, hostage logic and digital support interruptions.
The visual pack should stay cleaner and more blue/digital than the Shadow Moses Codec.
Future Side Ops could use segmented platform stages rather than a single linear dock route.

title: Mother Base
subtitle: MSF / Diamond Dogs management and briefing hub
summary: Best future hub for tapes, staff management, mission selection and iDroid-style support.
details: Mother Base should not behave like a classic Codec screen; it fits better as a management/tape/briefing hub.
A future pass can add departments, staff morale, mission deployment and replayable Side Ops selection.
The current Tape Archive already lays the foundation for this module.

title: Mission 001 — Dock Infiltration
subtitle: Side Ops vertical slice
summary: First playable mission: side-scroller infiltration, keycard, alert states, searchlight yard, mini-boss, secrets and extraction.
details: This mission validates the fusion between Side Ops gameplay and Codec triggers.
It is a local simulation mission designed to test systems, not a direct remake of a canonical map.
The objective chain is intentionally compact so future missions can reuse and expand its systems.

title: Armored Guard Captain
subtitle: Simulation mini-boss / Side Ops systems test
summary: Original local mini-boss used to test boss phases, Codec hints, extraction lock and rank scoring.
details: The captain is intentionally simulation-only so early balancing does not depend on a major canonical boss.
His two-phase behavior tests player pressure, distance control, ration timing and disciplined fire.
Future boss packs can replace or extend this with era-specific characters once the gameplay shell is stable.

title: SOCOM Suppressed
subtitle: Default Side Ops firearm
summary: Core weapon for the first Side Ops prototype: accurate enough for action, limited enough to preserve stealth pressure.
details: The suppressor reduces the gameplay noise footprint but should not make shots consequence-free.
The rank system tracks shots fired, hits, kills and stealth score separately.
Future weapon training VR missions can reuse the same weapon discipline model.

title: Chaff Grenade
subtitle: Electronic disruption gadget
summary: Prototype gadget that disables camera/projector windows and creates a clean surveillance challenge path.
details: The current Side Ops implementation uses chaff as temporary electronic disruption against camera and searchlight logic.
It should remain timing-based: chaff creates a window, not a permanent solution.
The Chaff Breaker VR mission is built around this doctrine.

title: Codec System
subtitle: Shared tactical communication engine
summary: Shared engine for manual calls, incoming calls, memory contacts, custom conversations and Side Ops triggers.
details: The Codec Simulator and Side Ops both read the same conversation/contact data instead of duplicating dialogue logic.
Custom conversations from the Studio can override mission triggers locally.
This keeps the project modular: new missions can reuse old contacts and new contacts can appear in both simulation and gameplay.

title: Tape Archive / iDroid Deck
subtitle: Cassette, briefing and debriefing archive
summary: Archive module for tapes, transcripts, notes, favorites, VR rewards and future Mother Base content.
details: The deck supports simulated playback, waveform, transcript navigation and local notes.
VR Missions can unlock conditional tapes through local rank progress.
Future passes can connect Side Ops rank results to debriefing tapes automatically.

title: Rank Engine
subtitle: Mission evaluation and stealth scoring layer
summary: Scoring logic used by Side Ops and VR Missions to evaluate stealth discipline, speed, damage, kills and secrets.
details: The Side Ops mission result tracks alert count, kills, neutralizations, shots, damage, rations, secrets and boss status.
VR Missions have independent requirement-based scoring and unlocks.
Future improvements can unify both into one profile progression layer.

title: Conversation Studio
subtitle: Local Codec script editor
summary: Tool for creating custom Codec conversations, previewing them and assigning them to Side Ops triggers.
details: The Studio saves custom conversations in localStorage and can export/import JSON.
Trigger overrides allow a custom script to replace a built-in call for mission_start, first_alert, boss_intro and more.
This is the main authoring tool for private fan scenarios and future mission packs.

title: VR Training Layer
subtitle: Short challenge and unlock system
summary: Separate challenge layer for time attack, no alert, weapon discipline, CQC, surveillance and boss drills.
details: The current VR module is a scoring/training board, not yet a Phaser gameplay bridge.
It already stores best runs, recent records, badges and tape unlocks locally.
Future passes can connect each VR entry to compact real scenes derived from the Side Ops map.

title: Frequency Protocol
subtitle: Manual tuning, memory contacts and signal states
summary: Rules that keep old-school Codec tuning consistent across eras while allowing modern packs to use simulated channels.
details: Classic eras use exact or near-exact frequency matching and Memory contacts.
Modern packs such as MGSV/iDroid should be treated as simulated channels or tape entries instead of forcing classic frequencies.
Signal states include stable, weak, jammed, encrypted, unknown and Patriots corrupt.

title: Mission 002 — Tanker Hold Sabotage
subtitle: Second playable Side Ops mission
summary: Second Side Ops route, built as a MGS2-era simulation with rain deck traversal, bulkhead lock, searchlight zone, and a shielded commander boss.
details: Introduces selectable Side Ops missions instead of a single hard-coded dock route.
Uses Otacon MGS2 as the main support contact for briefing, warnings, boss hints, and completion.
Stores best rank locally per mission, allowing Dock and Tanker routes to be replayed independently.

title: Shielded Deck Commander
subtitle: Mission 002 boss
summary: Side Ops boss designed to test repositioning, cover timing, CQC risk, and chaff-assisted movement in the tanker mission pack.
details: The boss has more health than the first Armored Guard Captain and uses a faster second phase.
The fight locks the cargo hold exit until defeated.
Codec guidance is routed through Otacon's MGS2 support channel.

title: VR Phaser Bridge
subtitle: Playable VR mission bridge
summary: System layer that turns VR Mission definitions into compact playable Phaser arenas and routes the resulting performance stats back into the VR rank and reward engine.
details: The bridge reads the selected VR mission from localStorage and starts VRTrainingScene through the shared Phaser config.
It supports Time Attack, No Alert, Weapon Training, CQC, Surveillance and Boss Challenge variants using the same input doctrine as Side Ops.
The scene emits live stats and completion payloads through the global GameEvents bus so the React VR screen can evaluate real play rather than mock training-board values.

title: Side Ops Mission Builder
subtitle: Local mission authoring and content-pack pipeline
summary: Editor layer that creates Side Ops missions from placed entities, validates their required gameplay flow, and converts them into profiles consumed by the existing Phaser runtime.
details: Mission documents store metadata, objectives, Codec trigger routes, entities, starting resources and publication state in a versioned local library.
The stage editor supports placement and dragging of platforms, crates, guards, security devices, pickups, secrets, a boss, mission keys and extraction points.
Valid missions can be armed for immediate playtesting or published into the normal Side Ops mission selector without modifying source JSON.
Single missions or complete libraries can be exported as dependency-aware JSON content packs and imported again on another installation.

title: Campaign Ops Progression Layer
subtitle: Connected operations, resources and cross-module unlocks
summary: Persistent campaign layer that connects Codec calls, Side Ops ranks, VR records, tape listening and Lore review into one operation graph with resources and unlocks.
details: Campaign nodes use prerequisites and minimum-rank conditions to create a sequential tactical operation without restricting the normal Free Play modules.
Rewards include XP, command points, intel, supplies, credits, tapes, contacts, dossiers, VR missions and Side Ops routes.
Three independent campaign slots support New Game, Continue, export and import while existing module records can be synchronized retroactively.
Field requisitions spend persistent resources to increase starting SOCOM ammunition, rations or chaff in every Side Ops deployment.

title: Campaign Builder System
subtitle: Branching operations authoring and ending pipeline
summary: Visual authoring system used to create custom Campaign Ops graphs with exclusive choices, prerequisites, rewards and alternative endings.
details: Campaign documents are stored locally and can remain drafts, be published to Campaign Ops, or be launched as one-shot playtests.
Each operation node can target Codec, Side Ops, VR, Tape Archive, Lore Database or an internal campaign milestone.
Branch groups make route choices exclusive per campaign slot; choosing one option blocks the alternatives until that slot is reset.
Ending nodes award persistent ending records and can use heroic, neutral, dark or secret presentation tones.
Campaign packs are portable JSON manifests containing dependency lists for missions, contacts, tapes, VR missions and lore dossiers.
timeline: Shadow Codec Ops v1.8 authoring layer

title: Campaign Narrative System
subtitle: Persistent briefings, debriefings, events and campaign dossiers
summary: Presentation layer that turns Campaign Ops progression into multi-beat briefings, persistent narrative events, full-screen branch decisions and replayable ending dossiers.
details: Campaign, chapter, operation and ending presentations are queued from live campaign progress and acknowledged independently in each save slot.
Narrative variables can store strings, numbers or booleans and can be changed by set, increment, decrement and toggle operations.
Variable comparison conditions allow later operations and events to react to consequences recorded earlier in the campaign.
The narrative archive preserves viewed scenes while the ending gallery records every conclusion discovered across New Game+ cycles.
Campaign statistics aggregate Side Ops, VR, Codec, Tape Archive and Lore activity into one operation dossier.
timeline: Shadow Codec Ops v1.9 narrative layer

title: Narrative Audio & Localization Pipeline
summary: Shared dialogue runtime for localized subtitles, era-specific audio filters, timecodes and portrait expressions.
details: Supports EN/FR with English fallback and a JA-ready data structure.
Conversation Studio exports subtitle tracks in SRT format.
Local voice files remain optional and user-provided.
timeline: Shadow Codec Ops v2.0 narrative layer
